response:
It is a haunting meditation on obsession, illusion, and the relentless pursuit of a dream that may never have been real to begin with.

Through the eyes of a young man who moves to a coastal enclave of immense wealth, we witness the spectacle of a mysterious millionaire who throws extravagant parties every weekend, hoping to attract the attention of a woman he lost years ago. The narrative is steeped in the glamour and excess of a bygone era, yet beneath the jazz and champagne lies a profound sadness.

The story asks whether we can ever truly escape our past or recapture lost time, and it paints a portrait of a society glittering on the surface but rotting within. It is anchored by a symbol of hope that glows across the water, representing the unattainable future we all chase, only to find that the past is always just out of reach. It is a beautiful, melancholic critique of reinvention, reminding us that we beat on, boats against the current, borne back ceaselessly into yesterday.
